item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sorry to be nitpicky but this

"…this effect abruptly ceases its effect upon the affected individual and begins to affect the other individual. This effect has not been observed affecting more than three individuals at a time."

is killing me, you used forms of "effect" and "affect" 6 times within a sentence and a half. maybe think of a synonym of some sort?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i thought of his song "Dare to e Stupid" cause there's a line that says "Mashed potatoes can be your friend."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I immediately thought of this when I read this. Pretty coincidental if you ask me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bill, you stated almost EXACTLY what I was going to say.

The only major complaint I have is inconsistent redaction. In the first section, middle and/or last names were redacted. Halfway down, the names given were complete, and then the redaction resumed later on. I don't have a problem with the last names being cut, but I do ask that you be more consistent in your method.

+1 while squeezing all the Charmin I can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to make a coffee achiever scp now. A kuerig that makes anyone that drinks coffee from it suddenly really ambitious would fit really well with the style of this website

item.title: Great timing
item.content:encoded: I was JUST thinking about how those lyrics were the only part of "Dare to be Stupid" that I didn't know the purpose of
And many happy returns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I KNEW IT
I saw the name on the list and said "Oh my god. He made a skip based on a line from Dare To Be Stupid."
And then mashed potatoes were my friends.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The name alone, I think, is what caught everyone's attention

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was going to be stupid. So so so very stupid.
And then I read the interview log.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the biggest problems with this article's readability is the repeated use of various forms of the word "effect/affect." I would revise for this repetition, as it makes reading your SCP feel like a chore.

I see this as a somewhat amusing SCP. However, I'm not entirely sure about the distinction between your use of the terms "the mashed potatoes" and "SCP-2981." Reread it as if you're a stranger reading it for the first time. It took me a while to process what you were talking about in the exposition of the article.

The interview drags on too long for my personal preference, but that's OK I guess. It also contains a couple formatting errors (i.e. not enough spacing between two paragraphs). I also didn't think the language used was consistent. One moment the guy is pretty relaxed, and then the next sentence reads like a police report. I'd revise for tone consistency there.
If I were you, I'd take out the rambling sentences about what Jackson did next, etc. You can summarize all this fairly concisely if you try.

The whole article just feels prematurely uploaded. The small errors throughout are just sort of annoying.

I've downvoted for now, but if you update it with improvements I'll change my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose it was okay. No vote.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: Request upgrade to "Keter" or "Elucid". JK, I love this SCP, it's epic!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't even think that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Take some wooden nickels! Look for Mr. Goodbar!

But seriously, I loved this one xD was hilarious and actually… Rather frightening at the same time. 0.o

+1 from me good sir. I'd +2 it if I could.

Also… Can you please stop pouring gravy on my girlfriend here? AND OH MY GOD WHAT ARE YOU DOING WITH THAT FORK? Don't touch Amelia!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if bees were potatoes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made an overused skip concept into an original new story, loved it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like my birthday dinner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cognitohazardous Mashed Potatoes deserve a place beside Keter Cakes in the SCP fridge. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be the funniest NK-Class scenario ever. "I tried to talk to my friends, but then friends were potatoes."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was whimsically amusing enough to make me love it! I can just see this happening, over and over and over again.

+1…youve earned it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because at least one person would think something about cannibalism. Plus who knows what it'll do to a person after ingestion. Maybe the effect would reverse, and observers would see the person as a bowl of potato salad and try to eat them. Mass cannibalism…infectious mass cannibalism, as the effect spreads exponentionally.

Dear God why isn't this Keter…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why don't we just have someone eat it and be done with this potato silliness?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god I think I am in love with this.

edit: 44.7% of people means we tested this on thousands of people. And given the nature of the item… I can kinda believe that. Mostly I am glad that number is not 100%.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this even more after seeing what inspired it. :D But this stands apart from other skips with the same effect via everyone seeing it as a different person, and only one person seeing it at a time. That's very clever and a lot of fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The future's up to you, so what you gonna do?

Incidentally, the video for the song makes about as much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the core idea and the final interview hilarious and good enough to warrant an upvote, but the majority of the article could some work for tone, grammar, and wordiness. I have a feeling it would have been great if you had sandboxed it first, but since it's already up on the mainlist I hope you'll do some lightning-quick revision to bring it to where it can and should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fresh take on a concept that I thought could not be explored in any way that had not already been done somewhere on this site. There are a few typos ("cazard effect)"?) but those are easily fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is better than I expected it to be. There are a few other SCPs that are perceived as humans or cause things to be perceived as humans, but you managed to make this one different enough from the others, so well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is another element of the anomaly a compulsion to use footnotes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Potato come back here! Haven't I told you not to bring your chainsaw outside

+1'd at

SCP-2981 is a 4.8 kilogram mass of mashed potatoes displaying a cognitohazard effect in which affected individuals1 perceive SCP-2981 as being a living person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this after getting the song "Dare to be Stupid" by Weird Al Yankovic stuck in my head. Specifically the line "Mashed potatoes can be your friends"

Probably the quickest I've made a mainlist attempt though it doesn't qualify as coldpost since I got feedback

Image is by Elliot Harmon and was found here
https://www.flickr.com/photos/elliotharmon/3068895134/
